title: LIGHTNING HAS NO SOLUTION
top_caption: Lightning is one of the leading causes of death for adult giraffes. Not predators. Not disease. The sky. It can't crouch. It can't hide. In 8 million years the giraffe never found a solution.
bot_caption: Every animal on the savanna finds cover when the sky turns dark. It stands in the open and waits for whatever comes next.

title: THE PEACOCK PROBLEM
top_caption: Darwin wrote that one glance at a peacock feather made him physically ill. The tail makes it slower, heavier, and easier to see. By every survival rule, it should not exist.
bot_caption: Evolution did not optimize it for survival. It optimized it for attraction. Those are not the same thing.

title: THE OCEAN TAXI
top_caption: The jellyfish survived 500 million years without a brain. The crab found the one weakness in that design. By touching the tentacles, it changes the direction of drift.
bot_caption: No fuel. No engine. No permission. It rides across the ocean on a body that cannot understand it is being used.

title: THE LAST INSTINCT
top_caption: The fox is letting a human hand get close. That looks gentle. It is also the exact instinct disappearing, the one that kept every wild ancestor alive.
bot_caption: We call it trust when it happens near us. In the wild, the same softness is usually how a bloodline ends.

title: THE COST OF BEAUTY
top_caption: The bird is carrying color like a warning sign. Every bright feather makes hiding harder. Every display asks the same question: survive quietly, or be chosen loudly.
bot_caption: Nature does not always reward the safest body. Sometimes it rewards the one willing to be seen.

title: THE QUIET HUNTER
top_caption: The cat is moving like the ground forgot to make sound. Nothing in the frame looks rushed. That is the trick. The slower it gets, the less the prey understands.
bot_caption: The kill does not start when it runs. It starts when everything else stops noticing it.
